description: PRD-3 v0 Teams. End-to-end identity flow that unblocks all downstream CRUD: an OCA invites a teammate by email with a Role (OCA | cx_engineer) and a DisciplineScope; the invitee receives a Supabase Auth magic-link email; on signup or sign-in, every unexpired matching pending invitation is redeemed, creating memberships, participations, and assignments rows. Discipline is editable post-accept. Discipline-gated RLS on TestProcedureInstance is intentionally deferred to the next slice (Equipment/Asset CRUD). See docs/prd-3-v0-teams.md and docs/adr/0002-user-project-assignment-spans-two-tables.md. GitHub issues #18-#22 cover the same scope at the slice level.

userStories.title: Auto-seed all four DisciplineScopes on project creation
userStories.description: As an OCA, I want a new project to be auto-seeded with all four canonical disciplines (Mechanical, Electrical, Controls, General Construction), so that I can invite teammates to any discipline without manual setup.
acceptanceCriteria: New migration in migrations/ updates create_project_with_discipline to insert four discipline_scopes rows per project: 'Mechanical', 'Electrical', 'Controls', 'General Construction'
Function signature is unchanged; only the body differs
After running the migration, creating a project via the dashboard form results in exactly four discipline_scopes rows linked to that project
Existing projects are NOT backfilled (out of scope)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create pending_invitations table
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a pending_invitations table to store unredeemed invites with a token, so that invitations can be issued and redeemed across signup events.
acceptanceCriteria: New migration creates pending_invitations table with columns: id (uuid pk), email (text not null), org_id (uuid fk orgs), project_id (uuid fk projects), role (text check role IN ('OCA', 'cx_engineer')), discipline_scope_id (uuid fk discipline_scopes, nullable), token (text unique not null), invited_by (uuid fk users not null), expires_at (timestamptz not null default now() + interval '7 days'), accepted_at (timestamptz null), created_at (timestamptz default now())
Unique constraint on token column
RLS enabled on the table; OCA of the org can SELECT and INSERT; nobody else can read
Index on (email, expires_at, accepted_at) for fast redemption lookup
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create redeem_pending_invitations function and wire into handle_new_user
userStories.description: As an invitee, I want every unexpired pending invitation matching my email to redeem automatically when I sign up, so that I land on the correct project with the correct role and discipline.
acceptanceCriteria: Failing pytest integration test test_redeem_pending_invitations exists (marked @pytest.mark.integration) covering: single-invite redemption creates memberships+participations+assignments rows, multi-invite-across-orgs all redeem, expired invite is skipped, accepted invite is not re-redeemed, no-invite signup is no-op
Invoke /tdd skill: write the failing test first, confirm red, implement, confirm green

New migration creates redeem_pending_invitations(target_user_id uuid, target_email text) RETURNS int as a SECURITY DEFINER Postgres function. For each unexpired unaccepted row in pending_invitations matching email: INSERT into memberships (user_id, org_id, role) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING; INSERT into participations (user_id, project_id) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING; INSERT into assignments (user_id, discipline_scope_id) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING; UPDATE pending_invitations SET accepted_at = now(). Return count of redeemed rows.
handle_new_user trigger is extended to call redeem_pending_invitations(NEW.id, NEW.email) after inserting the users row
Existing no-invite signup behavior is preserved (zero redemptions, no errors)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build InvitationService and POST /invites FastAPI endpoint
userStories.description: As an OCA, I want a backend endpoint that issues an invitation by writing a pending_invitations row and triggering Supabase Auth to send a magic-link email, so that the invitee receives a clickable link regardless of whether they already have an account.
acceptanceCriteria: Failing pytest test_invitation_service_create_invitation covers: caller must be OCA of target org (else 403), email-does-not-exist branch calls supabase.auth.admin.invite_user_by_email with redirectTo containing the token, email-exists branch calls supabase.auth.sign_in_with_otp with the same redirectTo, pending_invitations row is written in both branches
Invoke /tdd skill: write the failing test first, confirm red, implement, confirm green
backend/main.py exposes POST /invites accepting JSON {email, org_id, project_id, role, discipline_scope_id} and JWT in Authorization header
Endpoint logic lives in a new module InvitationService.create_invitation; the route handler is a thin wrapper
Endpoint generates a url-safe ~32 byte token using secrets.token_urlsafe
Endpoint constructs redirectTo as <FRONTEND_URL>/accept-invite?token=<token>
Endpoint returns JSON with the invitation id and a success flag
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build minimal invite form on /project/[id]/members
userStories.description: As an OCA, I want a form on a project's Members page to enter an invitee's email, role, and discipline and submit it to POST /invites, so that I can issue invitations from the UI.
acceptanceCriteria: New route frontend/src/app/(app)/project/[id]/members/page.tsx

Page renders a form with: email text input, role select (options: 'OCA', 'cx_engineer', default 'cx_engineer'), discipline select (four canonical disciplines fetched from discipline_scopes for the current project), submit button
Submitting calls POST /invites with the current Supabase JWT in Authorization header
On success: form clears, a toast or inline confirmation appears
On failure: error message rendered inline; form is not cleared
Members list and pending-invites list are NOT in this story (slice US-008)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build /accept-invite interstitial page
userStories.description: As an invitee, I want clicking the magic link to land me on a brief interstitial that confirms redemption and forwards me to the project, so that the invite flow feels intentional rather than a raw Supabase redirect.
acceptanceCriteria: New route frontend/src/app/accept-invite/page.tsx (not inside the (app) group since the user may not yet be authenticated)
Page reads the token query parameter from the URL
Page waits briefly for Supabase auth session to be established; once session exists, redemption has already happened via handle_new_user
Page queries pending_invitations (or memberships/participations) to determine the project_id the user was invited to
Page redirects to /project/<project_id> on success
If the token does not match any invitation OR the invitation is expired, redirect to / with a flash message 'This invitation has expired or is invalid'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build members lib with getMembersForProject and getPendingInvitesForProject
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a frontend lib that fetches the current members and pending invitations for a project, so that the Members tab can render both lists.
acceptanceCriteria: Failing vitest test in frontend/src/lib/__tests__/members.test.ts covers: getMembersForProject returns joined rows (email, role, discipline_name) for users with memberships+participations+assignments on the project; getPendingInvitesForProject returns rows where accepted_at IS NULL AND expires_at > now() for the project; empty project returns []; mirrors the shape of frontend/src/lib/__tests__/projects.test.ts
Invoke /tdd skill: write the failing test first, confirm red, implement, confirm green
frontend/src/lib/members.ts exports getMembersForProject(projectId) and getPendingInvitesForProject(projectId)
Both functions return typed shapes ready for direct consumption by the UI
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Render members table and pending invites on Members page
userStories.description: As an OCA, I want to see who is on my project and which invitations are still outstanding, so that I can manage the team and follow up on un-accepted invites.
acceptanceCriteria: Members page now renders a members table with columns: email, role, discipline
A second region renders pending invitations with columns: email, role, discipline, invited_by, expires_at
Both lists use frontend/src/lib/members.ts from US-007
Loading state is handled (skeleton or spinner)
Empty state for both lists has a friendly placeholder
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build updateDiscipline lib function
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a lib function that replaces a user's assignment for a project with a new discipline_scope_id, so that the Members table can support inline discipline editing.
acceptanceCriteria: Failing vitest test asserts: after updateDiscipline(userId, projectId, newDisciplineScopeId), the assignments table has exactly one row for that user+project with the new discipline_scope_id; calling with the same value twice produces exactly one row (idempotent); the old row is gone, not augmented
Invoke /tdd skill: write the failing test first, confirm red, implement, confirm green
frontend/src/lib/members.ts exports updateDiscipline(userId, projectId, newDisciplineScopeId)
Implementation deletes the prior assignments row for that (user_id, project) and inserts the new one in a single transaction (or uses an upsert keyed correctly)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Wire inline discipline dropdown into members table
userStories.description: As an OCA, I want to click a member's discipline in the Members table and change it inline, so that I can correct discipline assignments without leaving the page.
acceptanceCriteria: Discipline cell in the members table renders as a single-select dropdown showing the four canonical disciplines
Selecting a different discipline calls updateDiscipline from US-009
On success the table re-renders to show the new discipline value without a page refresh
On failure an inline error appears and the dropdown reverts to the previous value
Role column is NOT editable in this story (out of scope for v0)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
